generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize resolving identifier references and type mismatches reported by the AGREE validator.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure type consistency in expressions (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new AADL architecture to fix AGREE contract errors; focus on expression correction.
Do not attempt to place guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not introduce new identifiers without ensuring they are visible.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
Component type and implementation scope placement
